description.en: Question 1. What expression can be used to show 270,240 written in expanded form? Choices: A: 200,000 + 7,000 + 200 + 4; B: 200,000 + 7,000 + 200 + 40; C: 200,000 + 70,000 + 200 + 40; D: 200,000 + 70,000 + 2,000 + 40.

Question 2. There are 20 students in Mr. Clark’s fourth-grade class. There are 5 times as many students in the entire fourth grade as there are in Mr. Clark’s class. Which equation can be used to determine the number of students, n, in the entire fourth grade? Choices: A: 5 × n = 20; B: 5 + 20 = n; C: 20 × 5 = n; D: 20 ÷ n = 5.

Question 3. A bar representing 1 whole is divided into 3 equal parts with 1 part shaded. Which model shows an equivalent fraction? Choices: A: a bar divided into 12 equal parts with 4 shaded; B: a bar divided into 4 equal parts with 1 shaded; C: a bar divided into 6 equal parts with 3 shaded; D: a bar divided into 3 equal parts with all 3 shaded.
Question 4. What value can replace the question mark to make the statement 3 2/8 + □ = 7 1/8 true? Choices: A: 3 1/8; B: 3 7/8; C: 4 1/8; D: 4 7/8.
Question 5. If 30,000 is divided by 10 and then divided by 10 again, what will be the resulting number? Choices: A: 3; B: 30; C: 300; D: 3,000.

Question 6. A table titled Team Name Votes shows: Bears, 12 votes; Comets, 36; Hawks, 117; Stars, 39; Wolves, 108. Which team name received 3 times as many votes as Comets? Choices: A: Bears; B: Hawks; C: Stars; D: Wolves.

Question 7. A diagram shows a ray with endpoint G that passes through K and continues in the direction of K. A separate segment passes through points F, G, H, and J. What is the name of the ray? Choices: A: ray K; B: ray FJ; C: ray GK; D: ray KGJ.
Question 8. The measure of angle G is 20°. What fraction of a complete circle is the measure of angle G? Choices: A: 20/360; B: 20/340; C: 20/180; D: 20/100.

Question 9. Dawn needs to fix windows in her house. She must buy 3 feet of wood, which costs $7 per foot. She also needs to buy 4 pieces of glass. Each piece of glass costs $23. What will be the total cost for fixing the windows? Choices: A: $30; B: $90; C: $113; D: $176.

Question 13. Jason makes aprons. A bar representing one yard is divided into 3 equal parts, and 2 parts are shaded to represent the fabric he uses for each apron. How many yards of fabric, in all, will Jason need to make 14 aprons? Choices: A: 4 2/3; B: 9 1/3; C: 13 1/3; D: 14 2/3.

Question 17. Which angle has a measure of 45°? Choices: A: an acute angle between a left-pointing horizontal ray and a diagonal ray pointing up and left at a 45-degree-looking slant; B: a narrower acute angle between a left-pointing horizontal ray and a steeper ray pointing up and left; C: a wide obtuse angle between a right-pointing horizontal ray and a shallow ray pointing up and left; D: an obtuse angle between a right-pointing horizontal ray and a 45-degree-looking ray pointing up and left.
Question 18. The value of the digit 5 in 24,513 is how many times the value of the digit 5 in 357? Choices: A: 10; B: 100; C: 1,000; D: 10,000.

Question 23. «In December, a toy store sold 934 puzzles. Each puzzle cost $6, including tax. What was the total cost of the puzzles sold, including tax? Choices: A: $5,434; B: $5,484; C: $5,604; D: $5,684.

Question 24. Which figure appears to show a pair of perpendicular lines? Choices: A: two separate lines with different shallow slopes; B: a horizontal line and a vertical line intersecting at a square corner; C: two separate horizontal parallel lines.
Question 25. Which fraction goes into the blank to make the number sentence 2/3 < □ true? Choices: A: 1/6; B: 3/6; C: 3/5; D: 3/4.

Question 26. Which model shows an angle that appears to represent 45/360 of a circle? Choices: A: an angle inside a circle that is about one quarter of a turn; B: a straight angle spanning half a circle; C: a small acute angle inside a circle that is about one eighth of a turn; D: a reflex angle covering most of a circle except for a small wedge.

Question 27. The number pattern 5, 20, 35, 50, 65 follows a skip-counting rule. Which number pattern follows the same rule? Choices: A: 5, 20, 25, 30, 35; B: 13, 18, 23, 28, 33; C: 12, 27, 42, 57, 72; D: 15, 30, 40, 55, 65.

Question 28. An acute angle is formed by a horizontal right-pointing ray and a diagonal ray rising to the right; the opening appears a little less than half of a right angle. Which measurement best represents the measure of the angle? Choices: A: 36°; B: 42°; C: 138°; D: 142°.

Question 29. Kailyn read 24 pages of a book. Logan read three times as many pages as Kailyn. Which equation can be used to find the total number of pages Logan read? Choices: A: 3 × □ = 24; B: 3 + □ = 24; C: 24 × 3 = □; D: 24 ÷ 3 = □.

Question 30. A team of volunteers collected a total of $5,144 selling T-shirts at a charity concert. Each T-shirt was sold for $8. What was the total number of T-shirts the volunteers sold? Choices: A: 632; B: 643; C: 655; D: 668.

Question 31. Two fraction strips are each divided into 12 equal parts. The first strip has 3 parts shaded, and the second has 6 parts shaded. What is the sum of the two fractions? Choices: A: 3/12; B: 9/24; C: 9/12; D: 15/12.
Question 32. What is the value of the expression below? 28 x 42 Choices: A: 420; B: 816; C: 1166; D: 1,176.

Question 33. A line plot titled Lengths of String is marked in eighths of a foot from 0 to 1. It shows 1 X at 1/8, 1 at 2/8, 1 at 3/8, 2 at 4/8, 2 at 7/8, and 1 at 1. What was the total length, in feet, of string that Mario used? Choices: A: 25/8; B: 36/8; C: 48/8; D: 64/8.

Question 34. Which set of models is equivalent to the expression 2 × 4/6? Choices: A: 8 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 4, each divided into 6 equal parts with 1 part shaded; B: 6 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 3, each divided into 4 equal parts with the top 2 parts shaded; C: 3 rectangles divided into 6 equal parts, with the first 2 rectangles fully shaded and 4 parts of the third shaded; D: 8 rectangles arranged in 2 rows of 4, each divided into 12 equal parts with 1 part shaded.
Question 38. Dani has 45 marbles. She has 5 times as many marbles as Joe has. How many marbles does Joe have? Choices: A: 50; B: 40; C: 9; D: 5.
Question 39. When a certain number is divided by 4, the answer is 160 with a remainder of 2. What is the number? Choices: A: 38; B: 42; C: 638; D: 642.
